overview.tagline: A tranquil, upscale township with excellent schools and natural beauty
overview.description: River Vale is a quiet, residential township in northern Bergen County known for its excellent schools, spacious properties, and serene suburban atmosphere. Situated along the Hackensack River with mature trees and winding roads, River Vale offers an escape from the hustle of more densely populated Bergen County communities while remaining within commuting distance of New York City. The township attracts families seeking top-notch education, safe neighborhoods, and a strong sense of community.
overview.schoolDistrict: River Vale Public Schools (K-6) / Pascack Valley Regional (7-12)
highlights: Top-rated Pascack Valley Regional High School
Low crime rate and family-oriented community
Access to parks and nature along the Hackensack River
Strong township committee governance with engaged residents

realEstate.assessmentInfo: Properties are assessed by the Bergen County Tax Assessor's office. River Vale features predominantly single-family homes on generous lots, with a mix of ranches, colonials, and contemporary designs.
realEstate.recentTrends: River Vale's real estate market has been consistently strong, with families drawn to the Pascack Valley High School district. Homes on larger lots and those with updated amenities command premium prices. Limited inventory and strong demand have led to competitive bidding situations. Some older homes are being renovated or replaced with new construction.
neighborhoodNotes: Central River Vale — Established neighborhoods with mid-century homes near township facilities
Hackensack River area — Properties with wooded settings and river proximity
Rivervale Road corridor — Mix of residential homes and community facilities
Northern section — Larger lots near the Woodcliff Lake border
Poplar Road area — Quiet, family-friendly streets with well-maintained homes

trashCollection.title: Trash Collection
trashCollection.description: Curbside trash pickup is provided twice weekly by the Township of River Vale.
details: Pickup occurs twice per week on designated days
Trash must be placed curbside by 6:00 AM on collection day
Use approved containers with secure lids
Bulk item pickup available by appointment with DPW
Holiday schedule adjustments posted on township website

recycling.title: Recycling
details: Commingled recyclables (glass, cans, plastic #1-#7) collected weekly
Paper and cardboard collected on recycling day
Recycling bins provided by the Township
Yard waste collected seasonally from April through November
Composting encouraged through township programs

waterSewer.title: Water & Sewer
waterSewer.description: Water is provided by Veolia Water and sewer service is maintained by the Township.
details: Water supplied by Veolia Water (formerly Suez/United Water)
Sewer service maintained by the Township DPW
Water billing handled by Veolia quarterly
Annual water quality reports available online
Some properties may have individual septic systems

permits.title: Building & Permits
permits.description: The Building Department handles construction permits, zoning, and code enforcement.
details: Building permits required for construction, additions, and renovations
Zoning applications reviewed by the Planning Board and Zoning Board
Permit applications available at Township Hall
Inspections scheduled through the Building Department
Environmental impact considerations for properties near wetlands

parking.title: Parking
parking.description: As a residential township, parking regulations are primarily focused on street parking and snow emergencies.
details: Overnight parking restrictions on township roads
Snow emergency parking bans enforced
No metered parking — residential character maintained
Commuter parking available at nearby train stations in Westwood and Park Ridge
Township ordinances regulate parking of commercial vehicles

additionalServices.title: River Vale Free Public Library
additionalServices.description: Full-service public library with books, digital resources, programs, and community spaces.
details: Located at 412 Rivervale Road
Children's programs, teen events, and adult book clubs
Free Wi-Fi, computer access, and meeting rooms
Part of the Bergen County Cooperative Library System (BCCLS)

additionalServices.title: Recreation Commission
additionalServices.description: Recreational programs for residents of all ages.
details: Youth sports leagues and seasonal programs
Summer recreation programs for children
Adult and senior programs
Community events at township parks

noise.title: Noise Ordinance
noise.summary: River Vale enforces noise regulations to maintain its quiet residential character.
keyRules: Construction noise prohibited before 7:00 AM and after 7:00 PM on weekdays
Saturday construction allowed 8:00 AM to 5:00 PM only
No construction noise on Sundays or federal holidays
Leaf blowers restricted to certain hours and seasons
Loud music and amplified sound restricted after 10:00 PM

propertyMaintenance.title: Property Maintenance
propertyMaintenance.summary: Property owners must maintain their properties and grounds.
keyRules: Lawns must be maintained and grass kept at a reasonable height
Property owners responsible for snow removal from sidewalks within 24 hours
Fences must not exceed 6 feet in rear yards and 4 feet in front yards
Dead trees and hazardous limbs must be removed promptly
Swimming pools must be properly fenced with self-closing gates

pets.title: Pet Regulations
pets.summary: Dog licensing and leash laws are enforced throughout the township.
keyRules: All dogs over 7 months must be licensed annually with rabies vaccination proof
Dogs must be leashed on all public property
Pet waste must be picked up immediately by the owner
Excessive barking may result in violation notices
No exotic or wild animals permitted as pets

parking.title: Parking Regulations
parking.summary: Street parking is regulated throughout the township.
keyRules: No overnight parking on township streets between 2:00 AM and 6:00 AM
Snow emergency parking bans enforced — vehicles must be off-street
Commercial vehicles over 4 tons prohibited from parking on residential streets
Vehicles may not be parked on front lawns
Inoperable vehicles may not be stored visible from the street

rentals.title: Rental Property Regulations
rentals.summary: Rental properties must comply with township housing codes.
keyRules: Rental properties must be registered with the Township
Certificate of Habitability required before occupancy
Landlords must maintain properties to code standards
Short-term rentals not permitted in residential zones
Inspections conducted at change of tenancy

other.title: Wetlands and Environmental Protection
other.summary: River Vale has environmental protections for wetlands and riparian areas along the Hackensack River.
keyRules: Construction near wetlands requires environmental permits
Buffer zones must be maintained around wetland areas
Tree removal in environmentally sensitive areas may require additional permits
Stormwater management plans required for new development
other.source: Township of River Vale Code / NJDEP

schools.publicDistrict: River Vale Public Schools (K-6) / Pascack Valley Regional High School District (7-12)

publicSchools.name: Pascack Valley High School
publicSchools.notes: Regional high school shared with Hillsdale and Woodcliff Lake; strong academics, athletics, and extracurriculars

privateSchools.name: St. Andrew's Academy
privateSchools.notes: Catholic school serving the River Vale community

nearbyHigherEd: Ramapo College of New Jersey (Mahwah) — 20 minutes

whyMoveHere: Access to the highly regarded Pascack Valley High School
Spacious lots with mature trees and a semi-rural feel
Strong community with active civic engagement
Natural beauty along the Hackensack River

commuteToNYC.primaryMethod: NJ Transit Bus / Drive to Pascack Valley Line station
commuteToNYC.notes: River Vale does not have its own train station. Residents typically drive to nearby stations in Westwood or Park Ridge on the Pascack Valley Line, or use NJ Transit buses. Bus routes provide service to the Port Authority Bus Terminal. Highway access via Route 17 and the Garden State Parkway.

costOfLiving.summary: River Vale has a moderate-to-high cost of living driven by its desirable school district and spacious properties.
costOfLiving.vsStateAverage: Approximately 35% above the New Jersey state average

pros: Excellent Pascack Valley High School district
Large lots and spacious homes compared to other Bergen towns
Beautiful natural settings with wooded areas and the Hackensack River
Strong property values with steady appreciation
cons: No train station within the township
Limited commercial and dining options within River Vale
Longer commute to NYC compared to southern Bergen towns
Car-dependent due to limited public transit and walkability

restaurants.name: River Palm Terrace
restaurants.description: Upscale steakhouse known for prime cuts, fresh seafood, and a refined dining experience. A Bergen County dining destination.

restaurants.name: Bianco Rosso
restaurants.description: Italian restaurant offering traditional pasta dishes, wood-fired pizza, and seasonal Italian specialties.

restaurants.name: River Vale Bagels
restaurants.description: Neighborhood bagel shop serving fresh-baked bagels, deli sandwiches, and breakfast items.

parks.name: River Vale Community Park
parks.description: Township park featuring sports fields, playgrounds, walking paths, and picnic areas for community use.

parks.name: Tice Property Park
parks.description: Open green space with walking trails and natural areas, popular for casual walks and nature observation.

parks.name: Hackensack River Park
parks.description: Scenic park along the Hackensack River offering fishing, walking paths, and peaceful natural settings.

events.name: River Vale Day
events.description: Annual township celebration with food vendors, live entertainment, games, craft booths, and family activities.

events.name: Memorial Day Ceremony
events.description: Annual ceremony honoring veterans at the township memorial, followed by community gatherings.

events.name: Holiday Tree Lighting
events.description: Annual holiday celebration with tree lighting, caroling, and refreshments at Township Hall.

attractions.name: River Palm Terrace
attractions.description: One of Bergen County's premier steakhouses, drawing diners from across the region for its exceptional beef and seafood.

attractions.name: Hackensack River Greenway
attractions.description: Scenic trails along the Hackensack River connecting River Vale to neighboring communities, ideal for walking and cycling.

shoppingAreas: Rivervale Road — Small shopping plazas with local services
Westwood Avenue — Local shops and convenience stores
Westwood downtown — Adjacent borough with more retail options
Garden State Plaza (Paramus) — Major regional mall nearby

government.meetingSchedule: Township Committee meets on the 2nd and 4th Monday of each month at 7:30 PM
government.meetingLocation: Township Hall, 406 Rivervale Road, River Vale, NJ 07675

hospitals.name: Valley Hospital (Valley Health System)
hospitals.address: 223 North Van Dien Avenue, Ridgewood, NJ 07450

importantNumbers.label: Bergen County Office of Emergency Management
